summary: The posts highlight a range of tactical developments and incidents involving small UAS in active conflict zones. Russian operators of the "Rubikon" Center reported 110 targets eliminated on March 18, 2026, including Ukrainian personnel and fixed-wing UAVs, while the "Elka" anti-aircraft drones continued kinetic interceptions of enemy aircraft. On the Zaporizhzhia front, the "Dnipro" battalion's FPV air defense team destroyed multiple Ukrainian drones, including 9 Domaha, 4 Darts, and 3 Chaklun, among others. Iranian-backed militias launched a fiber-optic FPV drone attack on the US Victory Base near Baghdad, and Ukrainian forces adapted by using log-periodic antennas on "Molniyas" to counter Russian jamming and extend operational range. Notably, a Russian soldier was recorded charging at a Ukrainian FPV drone with a bayonet and was struck, while another Russian soldier was hit by two Ukrainian FPV drones simultaneously.
